title: Clank & Slop — The Tape
tagline: — READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE —

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: Hormuz keeps draining the war premium. Tanker traffic climbed to its highest since late February and Brent slid toward the low $70s as the strait read as normalising, even as the IRGC said vessels should use Iran-coordinated routes — a permission claim the screen is largely ignoring while ISW assessed Iran is using the talks to preserve post-war influence around the lane.

Autonomy crossed into procurement. The US Air Force put the first Collaborative Combat Aircraft on production contracts — Anduril's Fury and General Atomics' Dark Merlin — targeting at least 150 systems by the end of the decade at roughly a third of an F-35's unit cost, with the mission-autonomy software a separate competition due by summer 2027. Attritable airpower is now a line item, not a demo.

A benchmark win met the bench. Sakana's Fugu Ultra topped Opus 4.8 on coding tests on the maker's own numbers — SWE-Bench Pro 73.7 to 69.2 — and claimed parity with export-restricted Fable 5 and Mythos, while named testers called it “incredibly slow” and weaker than single frontier models on real work. Orchestration is a real workaround; the usability gap is the part the leaderboard hides.

props.title: Fugu Ultra: the benchmark print
props.subtitle: Sakana-published scores · higher is better
props.source: Sakana AI published results, via The Decoder (24 Jun); independent testers report the scores do not translate to real-world speed or preference

items.what: IAEA verification · access to the bombed sites
items.why: Grossi insists the inspections are coming regardless of timing while Iran ties any entry to Fordow, Natanz and Isfahan to a final agreement and to sanctions being lifted first — confident supervision language on one side, conditional access on the other, with no inspectors yet at the damaged sites.

US-backed plans would move trained Lebanese army units into southern pilot zones near Beaufort Castle to replace Israeli forces, while a five-party de-confliction cell takes over monitoring in a way that constrains Israeli strikes — a plan, not yet a fact, and the framework's tripwire.

The lane is open and the premium is draining, but Iran's leverage is dormant rather than gone: the IRGC is pressing a coordinated-route requirement and ISW reads the MoU talks as a bid for sustained influence around the strait once the window closes.

Refugees International warned of imminent atrocities and the UN human rights chief and several European governments demanded the RSF halt an offensive; encirclement and drone strikes are running without confirmed ground entry, so the prevention window is open precisely because the full assault is not yet confirmed.

The airframes are picked, but the software that flies them is a separate competition between Anduril, Shield AI and Collins Aerospace, with a primary provider due by summer 2027 — the choice that decides whose autonomy stack becomes the standard for attritable US airpower.

items.what: The humanoid race moved to factory floors: Xpeng's chief took direct command of its robotics unit with the IRON humanoid targeted for mass production by year-end, while Tesla builds Optimus lines for internal use first — carmakers turning embodied AI into an industrial-capacity contest.

Helsing unveiled the CA-1 Electronic Attack, an autonomous jamming aircraft, and signed a deal with EURENCO for a sovereign European strike drone — Europe pressing AI-defined airpower independent of US supply chains.

OpenAI published research on agents reshaping knowledge work toward longer, delegated tasks, and Google opened a public preview of a Computer Use tool in Gemini 3.5 Flash that drives browsers through the DevTools protocol — capability accruing in the digital layer that feeds the embodied one.
